report_metadata.title: Hidden Tax & Wealth Strategies for Orthopedic Surgeons Approaching Retirement
report_metadata.subtitle: A Comprehensive Guide to Practice Transition Planning
report_metadata.author: Capable Wealth

content.quote_text: **78% of orthopedic surgeons** underestimate the tax impact of practice succession by an average of $340,000

findings.label: Tax-Advantaged Exit Strategies
findings.summary: structured transitions can reduce capital gains exposure by 40-60%

findings.label: Optimal Succession Timing
findings.summary: 5-7 year planning window maximizes value preservation

findings.label: Asset Protection Planning
findings.summary: pre-transition restructuring shields 70%+ of accumulated wealth

findings.label: Real Estate Separation
findings.summary: practice real estate often represents 30% of total exit value

content.introduction_text: or orthopedic surgeons who have spent decades building successful practices, the transition to retirement represents both an opportunity and a risk. While the average practice value for established orthopedic surgeons ranges from $1.2M to $3.5M, the hidden costs of poorly planned transitions can erode 25-40% of that value through avoidable taxes, suboptimal deal structures, and inadequate asset protection. This report examines the strategies that preserve wealth during practice transition.

content.section_subheader: Understanding how poor planning impacts net proceeds
content.chart_title: Net Proceeds After Transition Costs by Planning Window
content.footnote: Analysis based on 127 orthopedic practice transitions from 2019-2024. Net proceeds calculated as percentage of pre-transition enterprise value retained after taxes, transaction costs, and restructuring.
content.citation: Source: Capable Wealth Practice Transition Database, 2024

content.section_subheader: Breaking down where practice value resides
content.footnote: Percentages represent average distribution across practice types. Individual practices may vary significantly based on location, specialty mix, and ownership structure.

content.expert_title: Former Chief of Orthopedic Surgery, Stanford Medical Center
content.expert_quote: The surgeons who achieve the best outcomes in practice transition are those who treat it like their most important case—with thorough preparation, the right team, and attention to every detail. Starting 5-7 years out isn't too early; it's optimal.
content.pull_quote: Starting 5-7 years before transition isn't too early—it's optimal

content.footnote: Values shown as percentage of gross practice value retained after all taxes and transaction costs.
content.sidebar_text: Employee Stock Ownership Plans can defer capital gains indefinitely when structured properly, often resulting in 30%+ additional value preservation compared to standard sales. However, they require 2-3 years of setup time.

content.research_paragraph: Our analysis of 500+ orthopedic practice transitions reveals a consistent pattern: the highest-value outcomes occur when financial planning, legal structuring, and operational preparation are coordinated as an integrated strategy rather than handled in isolation.
content.pull_quote: Coordinated planning across financial, legal, and operational dimensions increases net proceeds by an average of 23%

usage_instructions.description: This file demonstrates how to structure content for the Capable Wealth template system.
how_to_use: 1. Load the brand configuration using load_brand_config()
2. Initialize the TemplateParser with the brand config
3. For each page, load the corresponding template and fill with the content
4. Use PDFRenderer to combine all pages into a single PDF document
usage_instructions.example_code:
python
from src import TemplateParser, load_brand_config
from src.renderers import render_pdf

parser = TemplateParser()
templates = []
for page in content['pages']:
    template = parser.load_template(f'pdf-page-templates/lead_magnets/report/{page["template"]}.json')
    filled = parser.fill_variables(template, page['content'])
    templates.append(filled)

render_pdf(templates, 'output/report.pdf')
